title: fagraea macroscypha
intro: Fagraea macroscypha, also known as the fragrant frangipani or Kadupul flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is known for its unique, white, and fragrant flowers that bloom at night and wilt before dawn, often considered to be a symbol of love and impermanence. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Fagraea macroscypha can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have health benefits such as reducing stress, improving sleep, and supporting the immune system.
